title: lissera hispanica
intro: Lissera hispanica, commonly known as Spanish valerian, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Valerianaceae family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, due to its calming effects on the nervous system. The health benefits of Lissera hispanica include improving sleep quality, reducing stress and inflammation, and exhibiting antispasmodic and anticonvulsant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lissera hispanica include valepotriates, valerenic acid, and isovalerenic acid, which contribute to its therapeutic actions such as sedation, anxiolytic effects, and muscle relaxation.
side_effects: Lissera hispanica can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like hives and itching. In severe cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can cause liver damage and kidney failure. Some users may also experience headaches, dizziness, and fatigue due to the plant's toxic compounds. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Lissera hispanica or any other herbal remedy to avoid potential risks.
